strengths: 17+ years of broad engineering experience spanning high-performance systems (millions of req/s at Dunnhumby), cloud-native tooling, and full-stack product work
Active and recent hands-on AI work: MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, plus a decade of open-source platform building (GISCollective)
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing through public workshops and a FOSS4G conference talk, indicating some capacity to communicate technical ideas externally
concerns: AI depth is thin and very recent (~1 year); no evidence of building or shipping production AI products at scale or recognized expertise in the AI field prior to 2025
The MIT BS CS credential (Sept 2009–June 2012) overlaps with concurrent employment in Bucharest, Romania — a geographic inconsistency that warrants verification before advancing

Career trajectory is consistently individual contributor / craftsman with no evidence of director-level strategic advisory, executive customer engagement, or cross-org influence at the scale this role requires
response.justification: Bogdan is a capable, polyglot engineer with genuine breadth and a recent pivot toward agentic AI tooling, but the role demands a director-level AI strategist with recognized expertise, a history of advising enterprise customers, and ideally an advanced AI/ML degree — none of which are present. His AI work is limited to roughly one year and sits at the infrastructure/tooling layer rather than at the product strategy or advisory layer. The credential discrepancy at MIT is an additional risk factor that would need to be resolved before any further consideration.
